steps.title: Recognize we need the slope of the line of best fit
steps.content: The phrase increase in total fat for every increase of 1 gram in total protein is asking for the slope of the line. When you move 1 unit right on the $x$-axis, how many units do you move up on the $y$-axis? That's the slope formula: $$\text{slope} = \frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x} = \frac{\text{rise}}{\text{run}}$$

steps.title: Read two clear points on the line of best fit
steps.content:
Looking at the line of best fit in the graph:
  • At $x = 10$ grams protein, the line passes through approximately $y = 15$ grams fat
  • At $x = 50$ grams protein, the line passes through approximately $y = 75$ grams fat

steps.title: Calculate slope and identify the answer
steps.content: Using the two points $(10, 15)$ and $(50, 75)$: $$\text{slope} = \frac{75 - 15}{50 - 10} = \frac{60}{40} = \frac{3}{2} = 1.5$$ For every 1 gram increase in protein, total fat increases by 1.5 grams. That matches choice C — C is correct.
